_comment: GRC-parity block target manifest. Machine-readable source of truth for the status dashboard and the agent work-queue. Edit this (status, grc_block, params, notes); metrics are filled from verification reports by tools/gen_dashboard.py. Do NOT hand-edit STATUS.md. status: done | in_progress | planned | wont_map. tier: 1=feed-forward (autonomous), 2=stateful/loop, 3=new GRC block to build. Priority is tier-ascending then listed order.
_naming_principle: Names must match GNU Radio so users never guess the equivalent. (1) Each Kyttar block's GRC palette LABEL is its GNU Radio counterpart's label verbatim (e.g. 'FIR Filter'), keeping a 'Kyttar'/'kyttar_' marker ONLY where GRC namespacing requires it to coexist with stock GR. (2) 'grc_block' below names the EXACT GNU Radio factory (e.g. filter.fir_filter_fff) so the equivalence is explicit and searchable in the dashboard. A Kyttar block with no clean GNU Radio counterpart sets grc_block to '' and explains in notes.

blocks.notes: Reference example; fully documented. Single MULQ.
High-pass DC remover.

Verified vs filter.fir_filter_fff from 2 to 64 taps. SATURATES on Q15 overflow (production fixed-point semantics; GR float never overflows) via COEFFICIENT HEADROOM, NOT a per-tap or end-only clamp (INV-13). The cell ALU WRAPS on overflow and its V flag is NOT sticky, so a sum can overflow a mid-chain MACQ and wrap back into range by the final op -- an end-only (or per-cell) clamp then MISSES it and the output rolls over (a 40-tap all-0.5 gain-20 FIR on steady 0.9 rolled to a sign-flipping mess). Fix: S=max(0,ceil(log2 sum|coeff|)); scale every coeff by 2^-S before Q15 (Sum|scaled|<=1 => the accumulator can NEVER overflow at any tap or cell), then restore the gain at the very END (single cell: after the last MACQ; multi-cell: on the LAST cell after its final ADD) with ONE SATURATING left shift by S -- the only place a true overdrive overflows, where it pins to +/-full-scale. Normalized filter (Sum<=1) => S=0, a no-op identical to a plain Q15 FIR and bit-exact with GR. The saturating shift can't use a V-flag clamp (SHL clears V); it detects overflow in O(1) with a bias-and-shift test ((acc+2^(15-S))>>(16-S)!=0) and pins via 0x7FFF+signbit. Build-engine gotcha: a GOTO/branch targeting a {write}/{jump}-placeholder label is miscompiled to a stray output jump, so the restore branches to a REAL-instruction label and uses a two-path duplicated-{write} + terminal HALT structure. Budget: S=0 unchanged (single-cell ceiling 6, TAPS_PER_CELL 5; 20-tap=4 cells, 40-tap=8, 64-tap=13); S>0 caps the last cell to 3 taps (and single-cell to 4) for the restore, so a high-gain FIR may use one extra cell (40-tap gain-20 S=5 = 9 cells). Bit-exact predictor = process_reference_q15 (scaled wrapping accumulation + the final saturating shift + the cell-accurate wavefront sample-forwarding); the gate compares the DUT against it EXACTLY. DSP equivalence cross-checked on NORMALIZED taps (S=0): the ref == GR float clipped to Q15. <=6 taps single-cell; 7+ a chained partial-sum systolic wavefront (TAPS_PER_CELL=5) laid out as a column-major serpentine FOLD (INV-8/9/10, output egresses the LAST cell). Substrate fixes inherited: INV-6 (entry WITH params), INV-11 (multi-cell egress PortMap WITH params). Sweeps drive >2*ntaps random samples + a deep-cell mutation (INV-12); the headline overload test builds the 40-tap gain-20 FIR on the real route+simKYT path and asserts it PINS at +full-scale with no sign flip, and a WRAPPING (no-headroom) DUT FAILS the gate (INV-4). KNOWN LIMIT (guarded): 320 taps (64 cells) exceeds the 10x12 routing capacity (no free corridor); ~200 taps / 40 cells still routes.
Recursive: needs empirical/pole tolerance + zero-input limit-cycle test, NOT op-count floor.
Rate change: fractional/decimated alignment; compare on decimated time base.
Complex multiply; param parity = frequency in Hz, derive freq_word.
Param parity: frequency in Hz, not freq_word (worst-offender refactor).
LLR output; tolerance on soft values.
Proof-of-concept: works in the coherent BPSK RX demo but has NOT been through per-block GRC-equivalence verification. Param parity: samp_rate/sym_rate/alpha/ntaps.
Stateful gain loop; trim settling transient, behavioral metric.
Proof-of-concept: works in the coherent BPSK RX demo but NOT yet per-block verified. Carrier recovery loop. Lock-detector + tracking-error metric, not sample-by-sample. Param: add order.
Proof-of-concept: works in the coherent BPSK RX demo but NOT yet per-block verified. Timing recovery loop. Worst-offender param refactor (kp/ki -> sps/loop_bw).
Proof-of-concept: works in the coherent BPSK RX demo but NOT yet per-block verified. Decision output; BER metric, not NMSE on packed words.
Gated passthrough; state-dependent.
Bits -> constellation; deterministic, exact compare.
Deterministic given seed; exact compare, NO correlation (no meaningful peak).
NEW: complex gain. Trivial extension of GainBlock to I/Q.
NEW: single ADD with an immediate.
NEW: FM demod (atan2 of conjugate product). Common, high value.
NEW: mixer + decimating FIR fused. Workhorse channelizer block.
